activities_projects.title: Double Bar Graph Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare two sets of data on the same axes
steps: Collect the marks of two students in five different subjects
Choose an appropriate scale for the vertical axis
Draw bars side-by-side for each subject to compare their performance
Add a legend to distinguish between the two students
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops the ability to visualize comparative data
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of scale and clarity of comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Median of Even Data Sets
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the median when there is an even number of observations
steps: List the weights of 8 friends in kilograms
Arrange the weights in ascending order
Identify the two middle terms
Calculate the average of these two terms to find the median weight
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Mastery of finding the median in even-numbered sets
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and averaging of middle terms (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Representative Value Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Determine which central tendency best describes the data
steps: Collect the prices of 7 different snacks at the canteen
Calculate the Mean, Median, and Mode
If one snack is very expensive (an outlier), explain which representative value changes the most
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the impact of outliers on mean vs. median
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the analytical explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Probability Experiment: Dice Rolling
steps: Roll the die 30 times and record each outcome
Calculate the probability of getting an even number (Total even rolls / 30)
Calculate the probability of getting a number greater than 4
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies the basic probability formula to experiments

activities_projects.title: The Average Rain Gauge
activities_projects.objective: Calculate mean from larger data values
steps: Find the monthly rainfall (in mm) of your city for the last 6 months
Find the total rainfall and the average monthly rainfall
Identify which months were 'above average' and 'below average'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses arithmetic mean to analyze environmental trends

activities_projects.title: Frequency Distribution Table
activities_projects.objective: Group data into a frequency table
steps: Get the ages of 25 people in your neighborhood
Create a table with columns for 'Age Group', 'Tally Marks', and 'Frequency'
Group the ages into intervals (e.g., 0-10, 11-20, etc.)
Calculate the total frequency to ensure it matches 25
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns to organize large data sets into groups
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct grouping and tallying accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chance and Choice Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between 'Equal Likely' and 'Not Equal Likely' events
materials: 5 red balls and 2 blue balls (or paper scraps)
steps: Place the items in the bag
Discuss the probability of picking a red ball vs. a blue ball
Perform 20 trials and record if the results match your prediction
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that outcomes are not always equally likely
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical prediction vs. experimental data analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Data Interpretation Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Extract specific information from a given bar graph
materials: A bar graph from a textbook or news article
steps: Identify the scale used in the graph
Find the difference between the maximum and minimum values shown
Calculate the mean value of the data represented by the bars
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Improves data literacy and graph reading skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct extraction of information from the graph (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Weight vs. Height Correlation
steps: Record the height and weight of 5 family members
Calculate the average height and the average weight
Does the person with the maximum height also have the maximum weight? Record your observation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Introduces the concept of analyzing two related variables
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation quality and calculation accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Mode of Multimodal Data
activities_projects.objective: Identify cases with more than one mode
steps: Consider the data set: 5, 8, 5, 12, 8, 15, 20
Identify which values repeat the most
Explain what happens when two different values have the same highest frequency
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that a data set can be bimodal
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of multiple modes (10 marks)
